goal: Decompose the incomplete default denylist vulnerability into retrievable and composable CodeQL query units.

steps.goal: Locate the default denylist policy artifact
steps.description: Find the field, constant, or literal that defines the default value of the disallowed login modules configuration key. This step should bind the vulnerable default policy artifact rather than the runtime override branch.
steps.expected_output: A helper predicate that binds the default denylist definition or its referenced field value.

steps.description: Find the Set.contains-style membership test that compares the trimmed login module name against the disallowed login module set inside the JAAS validation logic.
steps.expected_output: A helper predicate that binds the denylist membership check and the expression being checked.

steps.goal: Relate the default policy artifact to the membership check
steps.description: Generate a fragment showing that the collection used by the membership check is derived from System.getProperty(DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_CONFIG, DISALLOWED_LOGIN_MODULES_DEFAULT), including the split, trim, and collect pipeline if needed.

keywords: local data flow from default value to contains qualifier
collection derived from system property default

steps.expected_output: A helper predicate that connects the default policy artifact to the denylist membership check.

steps.goal: Constrain the default policy to be incomplete
steps.description: Generate a value-filtering fragment that requires the default denylist artifact to omit the required blocked member LdapLoginModule. This step should encode the vulnerability-defining absence condition.
steps.expected_output: A where-clause or helper predicate that constrains the default policy to omit LdapLoginModule.

steps.description: Generate a fragment that keeps the query focused on the branch where the default denylist is actually used rather than a complete custom override of the disallowed login modules system property.
steps.expected_output: A fragment that ensures the reported check is tied to the default-value branch.

steps.description: Assemble the final from/where/select skeleton that reports the incomplete default denylist at the policy definition site, using the policy artifact as the alert anchor and mentioning the missing blocked member.

reference_query_patterns: select anchor on field or literal

steps.expected_output: A final query skeleton that reports the incomplete default denylist using the policy definition as the primary result location.
